statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

articles.headline: Sun take on the Sky on 7-game losing streak
articles.description: Chicago Sky (4-11, 1-6 Eastern Conference) at Connecticut Sun (2-15, 0-8 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Seattle faces Dallas, seeks to break 10-game skid
articles.description: Dallas Wings (10-6, 5-4 Western Conference) at Seattle Storm (3-14, 0-9 Western Conference)

articles.headline: Clark leads Indiana against Phoenix after 26-point game
articles.description: Phoenix Mercury (5-12, 4-8 Western Conference) at Indiana Fever (9-7, 5-4 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Bueckers fuels rally as Wings erase 15-point deficit in 4th
articles.description: The Wings exploded for a season-high 36 fourth-quarter points to rally from a 15-point deficit and stun the Sky 93-92 on Saturday night.

articles.headline: Li Yueru hits 2 late free throws as the Wings rally past the Sky 93-92
articles.description: — Li Yueru made two free throws with 12.5 seconds left to give Dallas its first lead as the Wings scored a season high 36 fourth-quarter points to rally from a 14-point deficit to beat the Chicago Sky 93-92 on Saturday night.

images.name: Paige Bueckers, Li Yueru spark Wings' comeback to shock Sky
images.caption: Paige Bueckers, Li Yueru spark Wings' comeback to shock Sky

images.name: Dallas Wings vs. Chicago Sky - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Dallas Wings vs. Chicago Sky, 06/20/2026

articles.headline: Mercury end 4-game losing streak with 93-73 rout of reeling Storm
articles.description: — Valeriane Ayayi had 18 points and 10 rebounds and the Phoenix Mercury snapped a four-game losing streak by extending the Seattle Storm's skid to 10 straight in a 93-73 victory on Saturday.

images.name: Mercury beat the Storm by 20 at home
images.caption: Mercury beat the Storm by 20 at home

articles.headline: Reese reaches 1,000 career rebounds as Dream defeat Fever
articles.description: In her first season with Atlanta and third in the league, Angel Reese became the fastest in WNBA history to reach 1,000 career rebounds, reaching the milestone in her 79th game, as the Dream earned a 113-96 victory over the Fever.

articles.headline: Mercury beat the Storm by 20 at home
articles.description: Mercury beat the Storm by 20 at home

images.name: Mercury beat the Storm by 20 at home
images.caption: Mercury beat the Storm by 20 at home

articles.headline: Dream set franchise record for points in a game in 113-96 win over Fever; Reese hits 1,000 rebounds
articles.description: — Rhyne Howard scored 24 points, Allisha Gray added 22, and the Atlanta Dream set a franchise record for points in a game with a 113-96 victory against Caitlin Clark and the Indiana Fever on Saturday.

images.name: Caitlin Clark nails heat-check 3 from behind the arc

articles.headline: Could box breaking be considered gambling or a lottery?
articles.description: A long-standing practice in the collectibles industry is under the microscope.

images.credit: Ronaldo Bolaños/Los Angeles Times via Getty Images

articles.headline: Olivia Miles has first dud as pro, but Lynx see chance to grow
articles.description: Olivia Miles was held to a season-low seven points on 1-of-10 shooting in an 81-75 win over the Valkyries, but the Lynx called the off night "a tremendous growth point" for the rookie sensation.

images.name: Olivia Miles [600x400]
images.caption: Lynx rookie Olivia Miles, middle, was held to seven points on 1-of-10 shooting Friday night but contributed two late free throws and a critical block in an 81-75 win.

images.name: Golden State Valkyries vs. Minnesota Lynx - Game Highlights

article.headline: Nogic scores 27 points, Mercury blow big lead before topping Sky 91-83
article.description: — Jovana Nogic scored 27 points and the Phoenix Mercury topped the Chicago Sky 91-83 on Friday night after blowing a 17-point lead in the second half.
article.linkText: Nogic scores 27 points, Mercury blow big lead before topping Sky 91-83

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Phoenix Mercury vs. Chicago Sky - Game Highlights) 2026/05/16 ESHEET

article.story: PHOENIX -- — Jovana Nogic scored 27 points and the Phoenix Mercury topped the Chicago Sky 91-83 on Friday night after blowing a 17-point lead in the second half. Alyssa Thomas and Kahleah Copper both had 17 points for the Mercury (2-2). They combined with Nogic, a 28-year-old rookie from Serbia, for the final seven points, four rebounds and two steals after the Sky (2-1) pulled within one point at 84-83 with 2:09 to play. Natasha Mack had 10 points for Phoenix, which went 37 of 41 from the foul line on coach Nate Tibbetts' 49th birthday. Rickea Jackson scored 29 points for Chicago, which was 19 of 23 from the foul line. Rookie Gabriela Jaquez had 13 points, but Kamilla Cardoso was held to seven. Skylar Diggins, who was averaging 18 points, failed to score before an eye injury ended her night in second quarter. The Sky took their first lead at 75-73 on Jackson's 3-pointer with less than six minutes to play. She also gave Chicago an 80-79 lead with 3:49 to go. The Mercury led 45-35 at halftime after going up 45-30 when Nogic made a free throw following a technical foul on Chicago coach Tyler Marsh. Phoenix pushed the lead to 17 at 54-37, but the Sky trimmed the deficit to 69-59 at the end of the third quarter. Up next Sky: Cap their season-opening four-game road trip at Minnesota on Sunday. Mercury: Play their third straight home game against Toronto on Tuesday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
